title: When Life Doesn't Make Sense
excerpt: The hardest seasons are often the ones that shape us the most.
content:
There are chapters in life that don't come with explanations. No footnotes. No clear reasons why. Just a season that demands more than you feel like you have to give.

I have walked through a few of those. You probably have too.

When life doesn't make sense, it doesn't mean something has gone wrong. Sometimes it means something is being formed — something you can't see yet, that will only make sense on the other side.

Faith doesn't always remove the confusion. Sometimes it just gives you permission to walk through it without completely falling apart.

Keep going. The meaning is there, even when you can't find it yet.

title: The Power of Showing Up
excerpt: You don't have to be perfect. You just have to show up.
content:
Some of the most impactful things you will ever do won't feel important in the moment.

They will feel ordinary. Unremarkable. Even insignificant.

But someone on the other end of your ordinary moment will carry it for years. A word you didn't think twice about. A gesture that cost you nothing. The fact that you showed up when it would have been just as easy to stay home.

This is not about grand gestures. It's about consistency. It's about being present. It's about deciding — again and again — that the people and moments in front of you are worth your attention.

You don't have to be perfect. You just have to show up.

title: Your Story Is Worth Telling
excerpt: The most powerful stories are often the ones we almost didn't share.
content:
I have heard a lot of people say some version of this: "My story isn't that interesting."

And every single time, they are wrong.

Not because every story is dramatic. Not because every story has a perfect ending. But because every story is real. And real is what connects us.

The most powerful stories are often the ones we almost didn't share. The quiet ones. The ones that feel too small or too personal or too unfinished.

Those are the ones that make someone else feel less alone.

Your story matters. Not in spite of its ordinariness — but because of it.
